v1:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds extension metadata (quarkus-extension.yaml and pom.xml descriptions) to the SSE and stdio transport extensions so they display properly on code.quarkus and Maven Central. The approach is sound, but there's a typo and a missing field compared to existing conventions.

POINTS:
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:14] Typo: "stanard input/output" should be "standard input/output". The word "standard" is misspelled.
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:1] Missing `description` field. The existing core extension at `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml` includes a `description` field ("This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily."). The new SSE and stdio YAML files should also include this field so code.quarkus uses it. For SSE, something like: `description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using server side events (SSE).`
- [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:1] Same issue: add a `description` field matching the pom.xml description, consistent with the core extension's convention.
v2:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds extension metadata (pom.xml descriptions and quarkus-extension.yaml files) for the SSE and stdio transport extensions. The changes follow most project conventions, but there is a typo in the stdio description and the new extension.yaml files are missing the top-level `description` field present in the core extension.

POINTS:
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:12] Typo in description: "stanard" should be "standard". The added line reads `using stanard input/output (stdio)` — fix to `using standard input/output (stdio)`. The sibling SSE extension uses "standard" correctly, so this is an obvious inconsistency.
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Missing top-level `description` field. The core extension at `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml` includes a `description` key (`description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily.`). Add a matching top-level description, e.g. `description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using server side events (SSE).`
- [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Same missing top-level `description` field as above — add a consistent description, e.g. `description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using standard input/output (stdio).`
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:5, transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml:5] Minor: the `guide` URL is `https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html`, while the core extension's commented-out guide uses `https://docs.quarkiverse.io/mcp-server/dev/`. The core guide is commented out (not yet published), so this is low priority — just verify the URL matches the intended documentation structure.
v3:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds metadata to the SSE and stdio transport extensions so they display correctly with descriptions on code.quarkus and Maven Central. The changes follow existing conventions with three issues to address.

POINTS:
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:14] Typo in description: "stanard" should be "standard" — fix to read "This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using standard input/output (stdio)."
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Missing `description` field at top level. The existing core extension (core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml) includes `description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily.` under the `name` field. Add a similar description here for consistency, e.g. `description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using server side events (SSE).`
- [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Same missing `description` field — add e.g. `description: This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily using standard input/output (stdio).` for consistency with the core extension.
v4:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds metadata (pom descriptions and quarkus-extension.yaml files) to the SSE and stdio transport extensions so they render properly in code.quarkus and Maven Central. The changes follow repo conventions for keywords/categories/status, but contain a typo and a likely missing field.

POINTS:
- [transports/stdio/pom.xml:14] Typo in description: `"stanard input/output"` should be `"standard input/output"`.
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Missing `description:` field. The core extension at `core/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml` includes a top-level `description` key (e.g. `"This extension enables developers to implement the MCP server features easily."`). Without this, the Quarkus extension catalog will have no description to display for the SSE transport. Add a `description:` line matching the pom.xml description (or similar).
- [transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] Same as above — missing `description:` field. Add a top-level `description` key for consistency with the core extension's YAML.
- [transports/sse/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml, transports/stdio/runtime/src/main/resources/META-INF/quarkus-extension.yaml] The `guide` URL is set to `https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-mcp-server/dev/index.html`, whereas the core extension's guide link is commented out with a different path. Confirm that this guide URL is correct and the docs exist; if not yet published, consider commenting it out like the core extension does to avoid broken links.
